title: Detached HEAD Surgery
subtitle: Lost in time, but not for long

briefing.narrative:
You wake up in the monastery infirmary. The nurse monk — who insists on being called 'Doctor' despite having no medical training whatsoever — is hovering over you.

"Bad news," she says. "You've been checked out to an old commit. Your HEAD is detached. Don't panic — it's a git thing, not a medical thing."

She consults her clipboard. "You're three commits behind the present. Any work you do here will be lost unless you create a branch to save it. Think of it as building a lifeboat in the middle of a time paradox."

The monastery cat is somehow in both the past and the present. Cats are weird like that.
briefing.concept: When you checkout a specific commit (not a branch), you enter 'detached HEAD' state. You can look around and even make commits, but those commits aren't on any branch — they'll be lost when you switch away. To save your work, create a new branch with git checkout -b <name>, which anchors your commits to a branch name.
objectives: You're in detached HEAD state — make a new commit here
Create a branch called 'rescue' to save your work
Ensure HEAD is on the 'rescue' branch

hints.text: Doctor Monk checks her notes: 'You're in detached HEAD state. Make a commit — any commit. Then create a branch with git checkout -b rescue to anchor yourself.'
A passing monk leaves a prescription: 'Create a file, add it, commit it, then git checkout -b rescue. The branch saves your commit from being garbage collected.'
The monastery cat scribbles on the prescription pad: echo "rescued" > rescued.txt && git add rescued.txt && git commit -m "Emergency commit" && git checkout -b rescue
